preview_text:
The statistical model rates Turkey as a modest favorite, assigning the side a 38% chance of victory compared to 31% for the USA. The draw is nearly as likely as either team winning outright at 31%, suggesting a competitive fixture where a single-goal margin or stalemate are plausible outcomes.

The spread across outcomes is relatively balanced. No result commands overwhelming probability; the largest gap between any two outcomes is only 6 percentage points. This narrow distribution implies an evenly matched encounter where neither side enters with a commanding edge, and late-game swings or fine margins are likely to determine the result.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket both diverge meaningfully from the model, favoring the USA at around 50% compared to the model's 31%. They also compress the draw probability to roughly 23%, well below the model's 31%. These sources rate Turkey substantially lower than the statistical forecast does. The dashboard presents both views without claiming superiority for either; bettors and forecasters can weigh the discrepancy themselves.
